div.head: 84. Memorandum From the President’s Deputy Special Assistant for National Security Affairs (Bator) to Secretary of Defense McNamaraSource: Johnson Library, Bator Papers, Balance of Payments, 1965 [1 of 4], Box 14. Confidential. Copies were sent to Secretary Connor, Charles Schultze, David Bell, Gardner Ackley, and Robertson.
div.p: As a way of getting a Presidential seal of approval on the revised balance of payments program, I proposed to the President (with Joe Fowler’s approval) that he authorize the release of a letter from himself to Fowler, as Chairman of Cabinet Committee, approving the recommendations of the Committee and instructing that we put them into effect. A copy of a draft which I sent to the Ranch this morning is attached.Not attached; a copy of a draft on the same subject, November 29, by Bator, is ibid., National Security File, Subject File, Balance of Payments, Vol. 3 [1 of 2], Box 2. This draft letter is identical to the version dated December 2 and released on December 5; see footnote 4, Document 83. If you have any comments or suggestions, please have someone in your office call me.See page 2— (on McNamara’s Memo). [Handwritten footnote in the source text. The memorandum has not been identified.]

The tentative plan is either to have a press conference led by Joe Fowler at the White House on Thursday morning, or simply to release the President’s letterSee footnotes 3 and 4, Document 83. and a sanitized version of the Cabinet Committee report,See footnote 5, Document 83. with no press conference but backgrounding by appropriate
members of the Executive Committee. We’ll know the President’s preference on this by Wednesday morning.

signed.note: Printed from a copy that bears this typed signature.
